fifo.name: Synchronous FIFO
fifo.description: A synchronous FIFO (First-In-First-Out) buffer with parameterizable data width and depth.
functionality: Stores data in a circular buffer with separate read and write pointers
Write operations store data_in at the write pointer position when wr_en is asserted and FIFO is not full
Read operations output data from the read pointer position when rd_en is asserted and FIFO is not empty
Maintains a count of currently stored elements
Generates full flag when count equals FIFO_DEPTH
Generates empty flag when count equals zero
Both pointers wrap around when they reach the end of the buffer
reset_behavior: On active-low reset (rst_n=0), write pointer resets to 0
On active-low reset, read pointer resets to 0
On active-low reset, data_out resets to 0
On active-low reset, fifo_count resets to 0
timing_constraints: Write and read operations occur on the positive edge of clk
Simultaneous read and write: count remains unchanged
Write without read when not full: count increments by 1
Read without write when not empty: count decrements by 1
safety_properties: FIFO should never write when full
FIFO should never read when empty
Full and empty should never be asserted simultaneously

arbiter.name: Round-Robin Arbiter
arbiter.description: A round-robin arbiter that grants access to one of NUM_REQ requestors in a fair rotating priority scheme.
functionality: Accepts request signals from NUM_REQ requestors
Grants access to exactly one requestor at a time using one-hot encoding
Uses round-robin priority: after granting to requestor i, priority shifts so that requestor i+1 has highest priority
Uses masked request approach: requests are masked based on priority, falling back to unmasked if no masked requests exist
Active signal indicates that any grant is currently active
reset_behavior: On active-low reset, grant output resets to 0 (no grant)
On active-low reset, priority_mask resets to all 1s (all requestors have equal priority)
timing_constraints: Grant is registered and updates on the positive edge of clk
When no requests are active, grant is deasserted in the next cycle
safety_properties: At most one grant bit should be asserted at any time (mutual exclusion)
Grant should only be asserted for a requestor that has an active request
If any request is active, exactly one grant must be asserted in the next cycle
No starvation: every persistent request must eventually be granted

fsm_controller.name: Traffic Light FSM Controller
fsm_controller.description: A finite state machine controller for traffic light sequencing with four states.
functionality: IDLE state: waits for start signal, transitions to GREEN
GREEN state: active green light, transitions to YELLOW when timer expires or no vehicle sensor
YELLOW state: active yellow light, transitions to RED when timer expires
RED state: active red light, transitions to IDLE when timer expires
timer_start signal is asserted on each state transition to start the timer
reset_behavior: On active-low reset, FSM returns to IDLE state
timing_constraints: State transitions occur on positive edge of clk
Output lights reflect the current registered state (Moore machine)
timer_start is a combinational output from next-state logic
safety_properties: Only one light should be active at any time
In IDLE state, no light should be active
State encoding should always be valid (only 4 valid states)
Cannot transition from IDLE without start signal
Cannot skip states (e.g., GREEN cannot go directly to RED)

signals.start: Start signal to begin light sequence

pipeline.name: 3-Stage Pipelined Datapath
pipeline.description: A simple 3-stage pipeline (Fetch/Decode -> Execute -> Writeback) supporting four ALU operations.
functionality: Stage 1 (Fetch/Decode): Captures opcode and operands when valid_in is asserted
Stage 2 (Execute): Computes result based on opcode (ADD, SUB, AND, OR)
Stage 3 (Writeback): Outputs the computed result with result_valid flag
Pipeline can be stalled by asserting stall signal (all stages hold their values)
Pipeline can be flushed by asserting flush signal (all valid bits cleared)
Operations: ADD (00), SUB (01), AND (10), OR (11)
reset_behavior: On active-low reset, all stage registers clear to 0
On active-low reset, all valid bits clear to 0
timing_constraints: Result appears at output 3 cycles after valid_in assertion (no stall/flush)
During stall, no data propagates between stages
Flush immediately clears all valid bits but does not clear data
Flush takes priority over normal operation but not reset
safety_properties: result_valid should never be asserted during reset
During stall, result_valid should hold its previous value
After flush, result_valid should be 0 within next cycle
Pipeline latency is exactly 3 cycles under no stall/flush
Data integrity: result at output matches computation on captured inputs
